title: Great learning environment with strong engineering culture
body: Great learning environment with strong engineering culture
pros: Excellent learning opportunities and technical exposure. Supportive mentors and strong engineering practices. Good work culture with collaborative teams. Exposure to global clients and modern technologies. Focus on continuous learning and skill development.
cons: Initial learning curve can be challenging. Project allocation may take some time. Workload can increase during delivery phases. Limited influence on project decisions for juniors. Dependency on client requirements and timelines.

title: Low Salaries and AI Craze
pros: Strong process culture and exposure to large, complex enterprise projects that look solid on a CV. Good internal learning resources, structured delivery practices, and generally smart, professional colleagues. Stable workload and predictable expectations compared to smaller, chaotic companies.
cons: Salaries lag behind the market, especially considering the workload and level of responsibility. Heavy hype around AI without proportional real investment, ownership, or compensation for people doing the work. Bureaucracy is significant, decision-making is slow, and individual impact often feels diluted.

body: Good opportunities for growth, but salary and internal training lag behind
pros: Opportunities for career growth and promotions over time. Supportive team culture and generally comfortable work environment. Bench management is reasonableГўВЂВ”people arenГўВЂВ™t immediately cut when between projects. Exposure to international projects and clients.
cons: Salary increases are limited, especially at senior levels; promotion does not always come with corresponding pay raise. Internal training quality has declined; fewer and less engaging courses compared to the past. Company focus has shifted more towards clients than employees; employee experience is less prioritized than before. Project availability can be challenging, especially in specific locations like Serbia. Typical challenges of outsourcing, including occasional project reductions and bench periods.

pros: I started at EPAM as a Junior Developer on a challenging oil and gas project, and with the support of my mentors, I grew into a Senior Software Engineer leading my own team. EPAM has always encouraged learning and personal growth through programs like the Culture of Togetherness, CSR initiatives, and hands-on trainings. During my maternity break, I received incredible support and flexibility, which made me feel truly valued. After 6.5 years, IГўВЂВ™m proud to be part of a company that genuinely cares for its people and inspires growth and innovation.

title: Strong engineering culture with excellent learning and career growth opportunities
body: Strong engineering culture with excellent learning and career growth opportunities
pros: EPAM has a very strong engineering-driven culture with high technical standards and best practices. Great opportunities to work on complex, real-world projects for well-known international clients. Continuous learning is encouraged: access to internal courses, certifications, knowledge sharing, and mentorship programs. Professional and supportive colleagues; teamwork and knowledge exchange are highly valued. Flexible work arrangements and good work-life balance compared to many consulting companies. Transparent performance reviews and clear career progression paths. Exposure to modern technologies, cloud platforms, and scalable architectures.
cons: Project quality and workload can vary depending on the client and engagement. As a senior engineer, expectations are high, and you need to be proactive and self-driven. In large projects, processes and approvals can sometimes slow things down. Compensation growth may depend heavily on performance reviews and project availability.

title: High Pay & Work Flexibility
pros: Competitive Compensation: Offers good pay, which is a major attraction. Work Flexibility: Many roles allow for work-from-home, though this can depend on your specific project. Positive Interview Process: Some candidates report a well-structured and positive interview experience
cons: Low Job Security: A frequently cited issue. Employees report high firing rates, short-term projects, and the risk of being laid off if on the "bench" (not assigned to a project) for 2-3 months. Constant Client Interviews: You often must pass new client interviews to join each new project, adding to job uncertainty. Project-Based Stress: The nature of project work is described as unstable, with cost-cutting measures leading to further insecurity

title: One of the better places that I've worked.
body: One of the better places that I've worked.
pros: Work from home. Allowed a lot of autonomy with regards to how I deliver on projects. Challenging projects.
cons: Management are forever kicking the can up the road with regards to discussions about salary increases. Pay has fallen behind inflation. Goalposts for promotion keep shifting; it's really hard to grow with the company. An internal talent mobility program exists, but I've never heard of anyone successfully get relocated; the only viable (and easier) option for switching countries seems to be to quit and change company ГўВЂВ” and EPAM operates in a bunch of countries that are becoming increasingly uncomfortable or downright terrifying to live in, particularly if you're a member of a marginalized group, and it seems that the company is either deaf or does not care. Relocation only happens if there's substantial business value for the company.

title: A Supportive and Growth-Focused Start to My Career at EPAM
body: A Supportive and Growth-Focused Start to My Career at EPAM
pros: I joined EPAM through the campus program, and my onboarding was smooth and well-organized. HR, staffing, and my practice leads were really supportive and helped me get into projects quickly that matched my skills. IГўВЂВ™ve worked on some exciting AI tools and a Code Migration project, which gave me the chance to collaborate with leaders and clients directly. What I like most is the open and supportive cultureГўВЂВ”itГўВЂВ™s helped me learn, grow, and feel confident in my work. Overall, EPAM has been a great place to start my career.
cons: No negatives; all positives here.

title: Workplace That Nurtures Growth and Leadership
body: Workplace That Nurtures Growth and Leadership
pros: IГўВЂВ™ve been with EPAM India for four years, and itГўВЂВ™s been a truly rewarding journey. The management here is very supportive and encourages you to take initiative, which has helped me build confidence and gain valuable exposure with clients and proposals. These experiences have been a big part of my career growth and eventually led to my promotion to Director. There are so many opportunities to learn and growГўВЂВ”through conferences, Tech Talks, trainings, and even organizing events like hackathons and quizzes.
cons: While I think some offices could use more space for recreational activities, Overall, EPAM is an excellent place to grow, innovate, and build your career.

title: Growing Together with EPAM
pros: I joined EPAM in 2020 as a Lead Data Engineer, and since then, IГўВЂВ™ve been able to work on some really exciting projects in industries like Life Sciences, Hi-Tech, and Consumer Brands. Along the way, IГўВЂВ™ve gained hands-on experience with tools like Databricks, Palantir Foundry, AWS, and Azure, which have helped me grow from Data Engineering into Data Platform Engineering and later to an AI Engineer. One thing I really value here is the Unified Assessment processГўВЂВ”itГўВЂВ™s transparent, guided by experts, and has played a big role in helping me shape my career path.

pros: Nature of Work and Projects: EPAM offers varied and challenging projects that really put your skills to the test. Work-Life Balance Work-life balance depends heavily on the project youГўВЂВ™re assigned to. With some critical clients, deadlines and workload might demand evening or weekend hours, especially closer to project delivery. However, these periods are typically followed by lighter phases. The company does encourage work-life balance, but sometimes client demands dominate.

title: Perfect work environment
pros: EPAM Systems is always looking after employee's wellbeing. They regularly schedule a meeting to check on your feedback and how you're feeling in your project. We get full health insurance paid by the company and we get a monetary aid to buy prescription lenses. The job is fully remote but they have very fancy offices with standing desks if you want to work from there.
cons: This is an issue you will find in any company that has this business model of assigning developers as contractors to their clients: You will find some unstability because if the client releases you from the project you will go to the bench and will have to basically do a full selection process over and over again until you get selected for a new project. If you're okay with that, EPAM is a great company for you. If you prefer stability, look for a direct job with a company that manages their own software.

title: Software Development Department
pros: Excellent learning platform. Work from home facility. Best medical coverage in industry.
cons: EPAM is primarily a body-shopping company with limited internal projects. Employees on the bench face high expectations, must maintain broad skills, and often struggle to get projects, which can seem arbitrarily assigned. The hiring process is rigorous with multiple technical, internal, and client interviews. Even after securing a project, many end within 6ГўВЂВ“1 years, forcing employees to repeat the interview cycle. Prolonged bench time can lead to being pushed into incubation programs, and continued rejections may result in being asked to resign. Potential candidates should weigh these factors before joining.

title: Life is unfair
pros: Nice people all around. Lots of upskilling opportunities. Somewhat interesting projects. Remote work.
cons: It's getting worse since 2023. Salary is not competitive. There are no relocations. There are no offline community events, only boring online ones. There are no business trips. The company has a skewed seniority pyramid with lots of directors who are toxic, incompetent and won't listen to people. The company doesn't respect its employees. There is a feeling of isolation and lack of vision or common goal. You are constantly being lied to about everything (promo, relo, benefits, etc).

title: Working culture
pros: Flexible Work Options Hybrid and remote work flexibility in many locations; work-life balance is generally good, especially in non-client-facing roles. Open and Transparent Culture Flat hierarchy with open communication channels. Leadership is often approachable, especially in technical teams. Focus on Innovation Encourages use of modern tech stacks, R&D, and experimentation. Good for engineers who like to stay on top of trends. Employee Recognition Regular feedback and recognition programs to reward high performers.
cons: Workload and deadlines can be intense depending on the client and project. Some projects may require overtime. Limited Onsite Opportunities (India-specific) Compared to service giants like Infosys/TCS, EPAM has fewer onsite opportunities, especially for mid-level employees in India. Slow Internal Mobility Changing projects or verticals internally can be a slow process and sometimes difficult without strong manager support.

pros: Good set of internal training courses. Certification costs are covered. Many skilled professionals work at this company. Plenty of opportunities for learning and growth. A good place to start your career. Covered health insurance, vacations and sick leaves. Equipment is provided. Assistance with obtaining a residence permit
cons: In Belarus, the benefits package was solid. Unfortunately, in Georgia it's significantly worse. Salaries are lower compared to competitors. Management is not always professional. Management often freezes salary increases, relocations, bonuses, and promotions for entire locations. After the freeze is lifted, no compensation is provided for the missed period. Relocation is only covered by the client. ItГўВЂВ™s hard to discuss financial expectations due to the constant uncertainty around freezes.

title: Good Pay and Benefits but make sure you are on a project
body: Good Pay and Benefits but make sure you are on a project
pros: The people are great, and the pay/benefits are good. They have a a nice upskilling program.
cons: Make sure you are are on a project, if not you are on the bench and have to "interview" for another project, it's like doing another job search for a job you already have. Also, in order to get a promotion you have to get an assessment by people that you have never worked with. It's a long process, that makes no sense. They will judge you based on questions and not actual project work.

title: Large and stable, but overly bureaucratic and lacking recognition
body: Large and stable, but overly bureaucratic and lacking recognition
pros: - International work environment with opportunities to collaborate on large-scale projects - Flexible working hours and the option to work remotely
cons: - The organization has too many layers of management with little real decision-making power, often serving as formal roles without true impact - Career progression is clearly defined on paper, but in practice, even when you're promoted, it often means more responsibility without meaningful changes in compensation or recognition - Employees who contribute the most often go unrecognized, while visibility and credit tend to remain at the management level - Legal minimums are followed in most areas, but the company rarely goes beyond that; benefits are average and only partially covered Overall, the system feels bureaucratic and disconnected from individual contributions or needs

title: Good company
pros: Working at EPAM Systems offers a range of benefits, especially for professionals seeking global exposure and continuous growth. The company provides opportunities to work with international clients across diverse industries, using modern technologies and agile methodologies. Employees appreciate the strong emphasis on learning and development, with access to internal training, certifications, and career advancement frameworks. EPAM is known for fostering a professional environment with a solid engineering culture, encouraging high-quality code and innovation. Additionally, many roles offer flexibility through remote or hybrid work options, making it attractive to those who value work-life balanceГўВЂВ”depending on the project.
cons: here are also notable challenges. Workload intensity can be high, particularly on demanding client projects with tight deadlines, which may affect work-life balance. Some employees report limited control over project assignments, which can lead to mismatches in technology or interest. The companyГўВЂВ™s size and structure sometimes result in bureaucratic processes that slow decision-making. While EPAM offers stability, compensation may not always be the most competitive in local markets, especially at the mid-level. Furthermore, the nature of a service-based company means that job stability can be influenced by client budgets and shifting priorities.

pros: Excellent company onboarding process. Great learning opportunities through various sources such as internal communities, training sessions, LinkedIn Learning, webinars, and mentorship programs. Strong company culture ГўВЂВ“ IГўВЂВ™ve had the chance to work with colleagues from various EPAM offices, and I genuinely believe that PEOPLE are one of the greatest assets of this company (though EPAM canГўВЂВ™t take full credit for that). Opportunities to work on different projects across diverse business domains. The office is well-located, offers a nice view and natural light, and has plenty of space for everyone ГўВЂВ” even though itГўВЂВ™s open space, it's usually around 80% empty. You can develop additional skills by contributing to activities such as mentoring, interviewing, assessment, pre-sales, and more. In some cases, these extra efforts are even financially rewarded. Remote work is generally allowed; office visits may be requested but are not mandatory.
